topic: Kernel-verified Lean proof closing an UNCLAIMED open sorry in Erdős Problem 257 (formal-conjectures)

artifacts.title: Lean 4 proof of Erdos257 tsum_top_eq (Lambert series ∑1/(2^n-1)=∑d(n)/2^n), kernel-verified

metadata.method: instantiate Mathlib tsum_pow_div_one_sub_eq_tsum_sigma at r=1/2,k=0; rewrite (1/2)^n/(1-(1/2)^n)=1/(2^n-1) and sigma0(n)(1/2)^n=d(n)/2^n; bridge ℕ+ to ℕ (n=0 term vanishes both sides); summability by geometric domination
metadata.unclaimed: no open PR/issue, no formal_proof annotation (verified 2026-06-14)

candidate_claims.assertion: The Lean 4 theorem `Erdos257.erdos_257.variants.tsum_top_eq` is proven and kernel-verified (axioms: propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound; no sorryAx): the Lambert-series identity ∑_{n≥1} 1/(2^n - 1) = ∑_{n≥1} d(n)/2^n, where d(n) is the number of divisors of n. Closes a previously-open, UNCLAIMED `sorry` (category: textbook) in google-deepmind/formal-conjectures Erdős Problem 257.
caveats: The textbook Lambert-series identity (a building block of Erdős 257), not the open Erdős 257 problem itself (irrationality), which remains sorry.
Kernel-verified locally; genuinely unclaimed upstream (no open PR/issue/annotation) so this one IS upstream-PR-worthy via the hosted-fork formal_proof route (83 lines > the 50-line inline cap).
